reason: REFUTED on a concrete blocking-recall regression. Verified registry.py line 91-104: application-logic's PRODUCTION block_threshold is 'info' (positional arg 3), and AgentSpec (line 41) confirms info<medium<high. The change sets block_threshold='high' and openly concedes 'Treat medium as advisory until a true-high bad fixture exists.' But the agent's ONLY bad fixture (tests/eval/fixtures/application-logic/bad/falsy-none-check) is the latent ORM-truthiness defect ('if not item' vs 'if item is None' on session.get), and all three baseline runs (findings/application-logic/bad/falsy-none-check__r0-r2.json) detect it UNANIMOUSLY at severity 'medium' (recall 1.00, fp 0.00; thresholds.yaml recall_min 0.90). Under today's info threshold that medium finding BLOCKS; under the proposed high threshold it no longer blocks. There is no high-severity bad fixture in the corpus (only falsy-none-check exists, expect.json carries no severity), so the change drops the agent's effective BLOCKING recall against its entire tested defect class to ZERO while leaving non-blocking recall cosmetically at 1.00 — a real correctness defect class (accidental-correctness/falsy-None sentinel) silently stops gating merges. The falsy/None bug is a genuine latent defect (the reviewers rate it medium correctly), so demoting it below the block bar is under-flagging a real class, not pruning a false positive. The prompt rewrite + fixture comment-fix are reasonable in isolation, but they ride the same change object as the block_threshold raise, so the change as written ships a recall-destroying threshold loosening with no high-severity fixture to justify it. Refute shipping until block_threshold stays at info/medium (or a true high bad fixture is added first).
